behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x ... optimal".
Slot B: Algorithm-quality check on deterministic inputs; no required external/module boundary in the behavior text.
Slot C: No level below Unit exists; the failure is inside heuristic logic itself.
Final label: Unit, based on Slot B (local algorithm boundary) and Slot C (no adjacent-lower level). Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess, AI output, or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function with deterministic coordinate sets (sizes 3..20), compute exact optimal length via brute-force/DP for tractable subsets, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5 for each case.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "computed correctly from the distance ... and the configured average speed for the road type".
Slot B: Deterministic formula + road-type speed selection logic can be validated within one estimator boundary.
Slot C: No level below Unit exists; a lower level cannot exercise arithmetic/lookup errors.
Final label: Unit, based on Slot B (local deterministic computation) and Slot C (no adjacent-lower level). Rejected Integration: behavior text does not require cross-module handoff failure to appear.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the estimator with table-driven segment fixtures (distance, road_type, speed config), then assert per-leg travel minutes and resulting window timestamps match expected values including rounding rules.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "validator rejects ... exceeds ... returning the excess amount".
Slot B: Single validation/math boundary (compare totals, compute excess) with no infrastructure dependency.
Slot C: No level below Unit exists; only local logic is under test.
Final label: Unit, based on Slot B (single-object validation boundary) and Slot C (no adjacent-lower level). Rejected Integration/System/Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is purely deterministic validator output.
behaviors.test_description: Pass total_estimated_time and remaining_shift_hours into the validator and assert rejection flag plus exact excess duration for boundary and overage cases.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "fails geocoding ... skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped".
Slot B: Failure is at module handoff: geocode result filtering -> optimizer input -> reporting output.
Slot C: A Unit test of only skip/filter logic can pass while missing end-to-end handoff bugs (e.g., skipped stop still reaches optimizer or report list diverges).
Final label: Integration, based on Slot B (cross-module orchestration) and Slot C (Unit misses handoff failure). Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network behavior is required because 'no results' is reproducible with an in-process geocoder fake; (b) no AI output dependency; (c) no multi-agent sequence; (d) no LLM-governed stage handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real service modules with an in-process fake geocoder client that returns coordinates for N-1 addresses and empty results for one; assert output route excludes failed address, optimization runs on remaining stops, and skipped-address report contains exactly the failed input.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: "consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice".
Slot B: Requires preprocessing + item-merge + routing modules to agree on canonical stop identity.
Slot C: A Unit test of dedup alone can pass while route construction still emits duplicate visits.
Final label: Integration, based on Slot B (multi-module state handoff) and Slot C (Unit misses downstream duplicate-visit failure). Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess needed; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-step agentic chain; (d) no LLM stage orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service pipeline in-process with duplicate address inputs carrying distinct delivery items; assert one consolidated stop in optimizer input/output, merged item payload preserved, and final route visits that address exactly once.
